vocabulary.meaning: a strong desire to do or achieve something
vocabulary.example: Her ambition is to become a successful doctor.

vocabulary.meaning: a formal request to an authority for something, e.g. a job; or a software program
vocabulary.example: He submitted a job application to the technology company.

vocabulary.meaning: an arrangement with a bank in which the bank keeps customer's money
vocabulary.example: She opened a new bank account to save money for university.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who is in charge of other employees or an organization
vocabulary.example: The boss asked us to complete the report by Friday afternoon.

vocabulary.meaning: an occupation undertaken for a significant period of a person's life and with opportunities for progress
vocabulary.example: He is hoping to build a career in software engineering.

vocabulary.meaning: a person with whom one works, especially in a profession or business
vocabulary.example: My colleague helped me prepare the presentation slides.

vocabulary.meaning: a commercial business or organization
vocabulary.example: She works for an international shipping company.

vocabulary.meaning: a written or spoken agreement, especially one concerning employment, sales, or tenancy
vocabulary.example: Please read the employment contract carefully before signing it.

vocabulary.meaning: a division of a large organization or business, dealing with a specific area of activity
vocabulary.example: He works in the sales and marketing department.

vocabulary.meaning: to be worthy of something because of one's actions
vocabulary.example: You worked very hard, so you deserve a promotion.

vocabulary.meaning: to receive money in return for work or services
vocabulary.example: She earns a good salary working as an accountant.

vocabulary.meaning: the state of being known or talked about by many people, especially of noteworthy achievements
vocabulary.example: He achieved fame and fortune at a very young age.

vocabulary.meaning: an aim or desired result
vocabulary.example: Our primary goal is to finish the project on time.

vocabulary.meaning: to make someone feel admiration or respect
vocabulary.example: The job candidate impressed the manager during the interview.

vocabulary.meaning: money received, especially on a regular basis, for work or through investments
vocabulary.example: The family spends a large portion of their income on rent.

vocabulary.meaning: economic activity concerned with the processing of raw materials and manufacture of goods
vocabulary.example: The tourism industry is vital to the local economy.

vocabulary.meaning: to ask questions of someone to see if they are suitable for a job; or a formal meeting for this purpose
vocabulary.example: The manager will interview three candidates tomorrow. / I have a job interview at 10 a.m.

vocabulary.meaning: the person who leads or commands a group, organization, or country
vocabulary.example: A good leader listens to the opinions of all team members.

vocabulary.meaning: a person responsible for controlling or administering an organization or group of staff
vocabulary.example: The manager approved our holiday requests.

vocabulary.meaning: a regular payment made during a person's retirement
vocabulary.example: My grandfather receives a monthly pension from his former employer.

vocabulary.meaning: the state of being extremely poor
vocabulary.example: Many charities work to relieve poverty in developing nations.

vocabulary.meaning: the feel of stressful urgency caused by having too many demands on one's time or resources
vocabulary.example: Students are under a lot of pressure during exam week.

vocabulary.meaning: existing or occurring before in time or order
vocabulary.example: Do you have any previous experience in sales?

vocabulary.meaning: a paid occupation, especially one that involves prolonged training and a formal qualification
vocabulary.example: Teaching is a very respected profession.

vocabulary.meaning: to leave one's job and cease to work, typically because of having reached a particular age
vocabulary.example: He plans to retire at the age of sixty and travel the world.

vocabulary.meaning: a fixed regular payment, typically paid on a monthly basis, to an employee
vocabulary.example: The company offers a competitive salary and health insurance.

vocabulary.meaning: all the people employed by a particular organization
vocabulary.example: The school staff are very dedicated and helpful.

vocabulary.meaning: a refusal to work organized by a body of employees as a form of protest
vocabulary.example: The bus drivers went on strike to demand better working conditions.

vocabulary.meaning: a compulsory contribution to state revenue, levied by the government on workers' income and business profits
vocabulary.example: The government decided to raise the tax on luxury goods. / The council taxes property owners annually.

vocabulary.meaning: having a great deal of money, resources, or assets; rich
vocabulary.example: They live in a wealthy neighborhood in the outskirts of London.

vocabulary.meaning: a doctor who specializes in treating diseases of the nervous system and brain
vocabulary.example: The neurologist examined the patient's reflexes and brain scans.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to cancel an event or agreement
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The football match was called off due to heavy rain.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to return something to its owner
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Please give back the book when you finish reading it.
EX: I wonder what is going on in the office next door.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to delay or postpone something to a later date
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We had to put off the meeting until next Monday.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to start a business, organization, or system
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She decided to set up her own clothing design business.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to go to bed later than usual
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The children stayed up late to watch the firework display.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to remove or carry something away
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The waiter came to take away the empty plates.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: to take control of something, e.g. a business or task
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: The large corporation plans to take over the small family business.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: right now; at this present time
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: I am a bit busy at the moment, so can I call you back?

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: responsible for or having control over something
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: Who is in charge of organizing the company conference?

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: traveling for work purposes rather than pleasure
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: My father is away on business in Tokyo this week.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: taking part in an organized refusal to work
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: The factory workers have been on strike since last Tuesday.
EX: It is important to arrive on time for your job interview.

prepositionalPhrases.meaning: performing or not performing one's regular work duties
prepositionalPhrases.example: EX: The police officer was off duty when he witnessed the accident.

wordFormation.meaning: assistant: a person who helps someone in a job / assistance: help or support
wordFormation.example: EX: He works as a shop assistant. / The charity provides financial assistance to families in need.

wordFormation.meaning: beg: to ask for food or money, or to make a strong request / beggar: a person who lives by asking for money or food
wordFormation.example: EX: The poor man begged for food in the street. / The beggar sat at the corner of the busy street.

wordFormation.meaning: bossy: always telling people what to do in an annoying way
wordFormation.example: EX: No one likes her because she is extremely bossy.

wordFormation.meaning: occupation: a job or profession / office: a room or building where people work
wordFormation.example: EX: Please state your name and occupation on the form. / Her office is on the third floor of the building.

wordFormation.meaning: employment: the state of having paid work / unemployment: the state of being unemployed / employer: a person or firm that employs people / employee: a person employed for wages or salary / unemployed: without a job
wordFormation.example: EX: She found permanent employment as a software developer. / High unemployment is a major economic issue. / The company is a very fair employer. / All employees are required to wear identification badges. / He has been unemployed for three months.

wordFormation.meaning: famous: widely known or talked about
wordFormation.example: EX: Paris is famous for its beautiful architecture and art.

wordFormation.meaning: officer: a person holding a position of authority / (un)official: relating to an authority or public body, or not formal
wordFormation.example: EX: A customs officer inspected our bags at the airport. / The team made an official announcement about the new manager.

wordFormation.meaning: retired: having ceased to work, typically due to age / retirement: the action or fact of leaving one's job
wordFormation.example: EX: My father is retired, so he spends a lot of time gardening. / We held a small celebration for her retirement.

wordFormation.meaning: save: to rescue or keep safe / unsafe: not safe; dangerous / safety: the condition of being protected from danger or harm
wordFormation.example: EX: The firefighter managed to save the child. / Walking in this area at night is unsafe. / Road safety is an important topic for school children.

wordFormation.meaning: success: the accomplishment of an aim or purpose / (un)successful: achieving or not achieving success
wordFormation.example: EX: Hard work is key to achieving success in life. / The new marketing campaign was highly successful.

wordPatterns.meaning: giving serious attention to what you are doing to avoid mistakes or danger
wordPatterns.example: EX: You must be careful with the glass cups.

wordPatterns.meaning: hard to accomplish or deal with for someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: It is difficult for him to understand advanced mathematics.

wordPatterns.meaning: annoyed or bored with a situation that has gone on for too long
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is fed up with her long daily commute.

wordPatterns.meaning: fully prepared for an event or action
wordPatterns.example: EX: Are you ready for the final exam?

wordPatterns.meaning: having control over or being accountable for something
wordPatterns.example: EX: Who is responsible for cleaning the kitchen?

wordPatterns.meaning: to make a formal request for something, e.g. a job or permission
wordPatterns.example: EX: He decided to apply for the position of sales manager.

wordPatterns.meaning: to rely on or be controlled by
wordPatterns.example: EX: Our travel plans depend on the weather.

wordPatterns.meaning: to tell someone about a specific topic
wordPatterns.example: EX: The company will inform all employees about the changes.

wordPatterns.meaning: to mention or direct attention to
wordPatterns.example: EX: You can refer to the dictionary if you don't know the word.
EX: She works as a primary school teacher.

wordPatterns.meaning: to be employed by a person or company
wordPatterns.example: EX: He works for a major technology firm in California.

wordPatterns.meaning: a type or category of something
wordPatterns.example: EX: What kind of music do you like listening to?
